query: You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository. The codebase is at version 2.175 (commit: 1f949804). ## Issue to Resolve Improve "discard changes" dialog options If you click Add note and type something and then close the window without saving the changes, the following message pops up. This pop up also appears at other places (for e.g. when leaving the Deck Options page without clicking Save). The issue with this pop up is that "OK" and "Cancel" don't seem to be the correct responses to a message that says "Close and lose current input". What about using the following instead? - Anki Desktop: - Google Photos: ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Will use `TR` from backend Good first issue, I have fixed it if anyone wants to drop a PR here's the patch ``` Subject: [PATCH] string from backend --- Index: AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/EditDeckDescriptionDialog.kt IDEA additional info: Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP <+>UTF-8 =================================================================== diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/EditDeckDescriptionDialog.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/EditDeckDescriptionDialog.kt --- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/EditDeckDescriptionDialog.kt (revision 906dd02571179f3096af4cdf32a924385c2af4b2) +++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/EditDeckDescriptionDialog.kt (date 1708346927147) @@ -24,6 +24,7 @@ import androidx.fragment.app.DialogFragment import com.google.android.material.appbar.MaterialToolbar import com.google.android.material.textfield.TextInputEditText +import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.withCol import com.ichi2.anki.R import com.ichi2.anki.StudyOptionsFragment @@ -106,7 +107,7 @@ } Timber.i("asking if user should discard changes") - DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog(requireContext()) { + DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog(requireContext(),getString(R.string.discard), CollectionManager.TR.addingKeepEditing(), CollectionManager.TR.addingDiscardCurrentInput()) { closeWithoutSaving() } } Index: AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateBrowserAppearanceEditor.kt IDEA additional info: Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP <+>UTF-8 =================================================================== diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateBrowserAppearanceEditor.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateBrowserAppearanceEditor.kt --- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateBrowserAppearanceEditor.kt (revision 906dd02571179f3096af4cdf32a924385c2af4b2) +++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateBrowserAppearanceEditor.kt (date 1708345145742) @@ -98,7 +98,9 @@ } private fun showDiscardChangesDialog() { - DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog(this, ::discardChangesAndClose) + DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog(this,getString(R.string.discard), CollectionManager.TR.addingKeepEditing(), CollectionManager.TR.addingDiscardCurrentInput()){ + discardChangesAndClose() + } } private fun showRestoreDefaultDialog() { Index: AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/DiscardChangesDialog.kt IDEA additional info: Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP <+>UTF-8 =================================================================== diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/DiscardChangesDialog.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/DiscardChangesDialog.kt --- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/DiscardChangesDialog.kt (revision 906dd02571179f3096af4cdf32a924385c2af4b2) +++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/DiscardChangesDialog.kt (date 1708344904076) @@ -23,15 +23,16 @@ import com.ichi2.utils.positiveButton import com.ichi2.utils.show -class DiscardChangesDialog { - companion object { +object DiscardChangesDialog { fun showDialog( context: Context, + positiveButtonText:String, + negativeButtonText:String, + message:String, positiveMethod: () -> Unit ) = AlertDialog.Builder(context).show { - message(R.string.discard_unsaved_changes) - positiveButton(R.string.dialog_ok) { positiveMethod() } - negativeButton(R.string.dialog_cancel) + message(text = message) + positiveButton(text = positiveButtonText) { positiveMethod() } + negativeButton(text = negativeButtonText) } - } } Index: AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/02-strings.xml IDEA additional info: Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP <+>UTF-8 =================================================================== diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/02-strings.xml b/AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/02-strings.xml --- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/02-strings.xml (revision 906dd02571179f3096af4cdf32a924385c2af4b2) +++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/02-strings.xml (date 1708345145711) @@ -94,7 +94,7 @@ Edit note - Close and lose current input? + Discard No cards created. Please fill in more fields The first field is empty This note type must contain cloze deletions Index: AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/NoteEditor.kt IDEA additional info: Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP <+>UTF-8 =================================================================== diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/NoteEditor.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/NoteEditor.kt --- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/NoteEditor.kt (revision 906dd02571179f3096af4cdf32a924385c2af4b2) +++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/NoteEditor.kt (date 1708345145736) @@ -1260,7 +1260,7 @@ } private fun showDiscardChangesDialog() { - DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog(this) { + DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog(this,getString(R.string.discard), TR.addingKeepEditing(), TR.addingDiscardCurrentInput()) { Timber.i("NoteEditor:: OK button pressed to confirm discard changes") closeNoteEditor() } Index: AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateEditor.kt IDEA additional info: Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP <+>UTF-8 =================================================================== diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateEditor.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateEditor.kt --- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateEditor.kt (revision 906dd02571179f3096af4cdf32a924385c2af4b2) +++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateEditor.kt (date 1708345145724) @@ -197,7 +197,7 @@ return tempModel != null && tempModel!!.notetype.toString() != oldModel.toString() } - private fun showDiscardChangesDialog() = DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog(this) { + private fun showDiscardChangesDialog() = DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog(this,getString(R.string.discard), CollectionManager.TR.addingKeepEditing(), CollectionManager.TR.addingDiscardCurrentInput()) { Timber.i("TemplateEditor:: OK button pressed to confirm discard changes") // Clear the edited model from any cache files, and clear it from this objects memory to discard changes CardTemplateNotetype.clearTempModelFiles() Index: AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateEditorTest.kt IDEA additional info: Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP <+>UTF-8 =================================================================== diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateEditorTest.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateEditorTest.kt --- a/AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateEditorTest.kt (revision 906dd02571179f3096af4cdf32a924385c2af4b2) +++ b/AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateEditorTest.kt (date 1708345337078) @@ -78,14 +78,14 @@ // Make sure we get a confirmation dialog if we hit the back button assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(android.R.id.home)) advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep() - assertEquals("Wrong dialog shown?", getAlertDialogText(true), getResourceString(R.string.discard_unsaved_changes)) + assertEquals("Wrong dialog shown?", getAlertDialogText(true), "Discard current input?") clickAlertDialogButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_NEGATIVE, false) advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep() assertTrue("model change not preserved despite canceling back button?", testEditor.modelHasChanged()) // Make sure we things are cleared out after a cancel assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(android.R.id.home)) - assertEquals("Wrong dialog shown?", getAlertDialogText(true), getResourceString(R.string.discard_unsaved_changes)) + assertEquals("Wrong dialog shown?", getAlertDialogText(true), "Discard current input?") clickAlertDialogButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE, false) advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep() assertFalse("model change not cleared despite discarding changes?", testEditor.modelHasChanged()) Index: AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/DeckOptions.kt IDEA additional info: Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP <+>UTF-8 =================================================================== diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/DeckOptions.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/DeckOptions.kt --- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/DeckOptions.kt (revision 906dd02571179f3096af4cdf32a924385c2af4b2) +++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/DeckOptions.kt (date 1708345145717) @@ -55,7 +55,7 @@ private val onBackSaveCallback = object : OnBackPressedCallback(true) { override fun handleOnBackPressed() { Timber.v("DeckOptions: showing 'discard changes'") - DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog(requireContext()) { + DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog(requireContext(),getString(R.string.discard), CollectionManager.TR.addingKeepEditing(), CollectionManager.TR.addingDiscardCurrentInput()) { Timber.i("OK button pressed to confirm discard changes") this.isEnabled = false requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed() ``` working on it ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateBrowserAppearanceEditor.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/DiscardChangesDialog.kt` - `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/02-strings.xml` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 37→
 38→@NeedsTest("pressing back: icon + button should go to the previous screen")
 39→@NeedsTest("15130: pressing back: icon + button should return to options if the manual is open")
 40→@NeedsTest("saveAndExit closes screen")
 41→class DeckOptions : PageFragment() {
 42→    override val title: String
 43→        get() = resources.getString(R.string.menu__deck_options)
 44→    override val pageName = "deck-options"
 45→
 46→    // handle going back from the manual
 47→    private val onBackCallback = object : OnBackPressedCallback(false) {
 48→        override fun handleOnBackPressed() {
 49→            Timber.v("webView: navigating back")
 50→            webView.goBack()
 51→        }
 52→    }
 53→
 54→    // HACK: this is enabled unconditionally as we currently cannot get the 'changed' status
 55→    private val onBackSaveCallback = object : OnBackPressedCallback(true) {
 56→        override fun handleOnBackPressed() {
 57→            Timber.v("DeckOptions: showing 'discard changes'")
 58→            DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog(requireContext()) {
 59→                Timber.i("OK button pressed to confirm discard changes")
 60→                this.isEnabled = false
 61→                requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed()
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    override fun onCreateWebViewClient(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): PageWebViewClient {
 67→        val deckId = arguments?.getLong(ARG_DECK_ID)
 68→            ?: throw Exception("missing deck ID")
 69→
 70→        requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.addCallback(this, onBackSaveCallback)
 71→        requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.addCallback(this, onBackCallback)
 72→        return DeckOptionsWebClient(deckId).apply {
 73→            onPageFinishedCallback = OnPageFinishedCallback { view ->
 74→                Timber.v("canGoBack: %b", view.canGoBack())
 75→                onBackCallback.isEnabled = view.canGoBack()
 76→            }
 77→        }
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    @Suppress("unused")
 81→    fun saveAndExit() {
 82→        // dispatch Ctrl+Enter
 83→        val downEvent = KeyEvent(0, 0, KeyEvent.ACTION_DOWN, KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, 0, KeyEvent.META_CTRL_ON)
 84→        val upEvent = KeyEvent(0, 0, KeyEvent.ACTION_UP, KeyEvent.KEYCODE_ENTER, 0, KeyEvent.META_CTRL_ON)
 85→        webView.dispatchKeyEvent(downEvent)
 86→        webView.dispatchKeyEvent(upEvent)
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    class DeckOptionsWebClient(val deckId: Long) : PageWebViewClient() {
 90→        override val promiseToWaitFor: String
 91→            get() = "\$deckOptions"
 92→
 93→        override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
 94→            // from upstream: https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/678c354fed4d98c0a8ef84fb7981ee085bd744a7/qt/aqt/deckoptions.py#L55
 95→            view!!.evaluateJavascript("const \$deckOptions = anki.setupDeckOptions($deckId);") {
 96→                super.onPageFinished(view, url)
 97→            }
 98→        }
 99→    }

 43→@RunWith(AndroidJUnit4::class)
 44→class CardTemplateEditorTest : RobolectricTest() {
 45→    @Test
 46→    @Throws(Exception::class)
 47→    fun testEditTemplateContents() {
 48→        val modelName = "Basic"
 49→
 50→        // Start the CardTemplateEditor with a specific model, and make sure the model starts unchanged
 51→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
 52→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
 53→        intent.putExtra("modelId", collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"))
 54→        var templateEditorController = Robolectric.buildActivity(CardTemplateEditor::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
 55→        saveControllerForCleanup(templateEditorController)
 56→        var testEditor = templateEditorController.get()
 57→        assertFalse("Model should not have changed yet", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
 58→
 59→        // Change the model and make sure it registers as changed, but the database is unchanged
 60→        var templateFront = testEditor.findViewById<EditText>(R.id.editor_editText)
 61→        val testModelQfmtEdit = "!@#$%^&*TEST*&^%$#@!"
 62→        templateFront.text.append(testModelQfmtEdit)
 63→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
 64→        assertTrue("Model did not change after edit?", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
 65→        assertEquals("Change already in database?", collectionBasicModelOriginal.toString().trim { it <= ' ' }, getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName).toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
 66→
 67→        // Kill and restart the Activity, make sure model edit is preserved
 68→        val outBundle = Bundle()
 69→        templateEditorController.saveInstanceState(outBundle)
 70→        templateEditorController.pause().stop().destroy()
 71→        templateEditorController = Robolectric.buildActivity(CardTemplateEditor::class.java).create(outBundle).start().resume().visible()
 72→        saveControllerForCleanup(templateEditorController)
 73→        testEditor = templateEditorController.get()
 74→        var shadowTestEditor = shadowOf(testEditor)
 75→        assertTrue("model change not preserved across activity lifecycle?", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
 76→        assertEquals("Change already in database?", collectionBasicModelOriginal.toString().trim { it <= ' ' }, getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName).toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
 77→
 78→        // Make sure we get a confirmation dialog if we hit the back button
 79→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(android.R.id.home))
 80→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
 81→        assertEquals("Wrong dialog shown?", getAlertDialogText(true), getResourceString(R.string.discard_unsaved_changes))
 82→        clickAlertDialogButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_NEGATIVE, false)
 83→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
 84→        assertTrue("model change not preserved despite canceling back button?", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
 85→
 86→        // Make sure we things are cleared out after a cancel
 87→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(android.R.id.home))
 88→        assertEquals("Wrong dialog shown?", getAlertDialogText(true), getResourceString(R.string.discard_unsaved_changes))
 89→        clickAlertDialogButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE, false)
 90→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
 91→        assertFalse("model change not cleared despite discarding changes?", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
 92→
 93→        // Get going for content edit assertions again...
 94→        templateEditorController = Robolectric.buildActivity(CardTemplateEditor::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
 95→        saveControllerForCleanup(templateEditorController)
 96→        testEditor = templateEditorController.get()
 97→        shadowTestEditor = shadowOf(testEditor)
 98→        templateFront = testEditor.findViewById(R.id.editor_editText)
 99→        templateFront.text.append(testModelQfmtEdit)
100→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
101→        assertTrue("Model did not change after edit?", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
102→
103→        // Make sure we pass the edit to the Previewer
104→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_preview))
105→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
106→        val startedIntent = shadowTestEditor.nextStartedActivity
107→        val shadowIntent = shadowOf(startedIntent)
108→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
109→        assertEquals("Previewer not started?", CardTemplatePreviewer::class.java.name, shadowIntent.intentClass.name)
110→        assertNotNull("intent did not have model JSON filename?", startedIntent.getStringExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME))
111→        assertNotEquals("Model sent to Previewer is unchanged?", testEditor.tempModel?.notetype, CardTemplateNotetype.getTempModel(startedIntent.getStringExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME)!!))
112→        assertEquals("Change already in database?", collectionBasicModelOriginal.toString().trim { it <= ' ' }, getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName).toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
113→        shadowTestEditor.receiveResult(startedIntent, Activity.RESULT_OK, Intent())
114→
115→        // Save the template then fetch it from the collection to see if it was saved correctly
116→        val testEditorModelEdited = testEditor.tempModel?.notetype
117→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
118→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_confirm))
119→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
120→        val collectionBasicModelCopyEdited = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
121→        assertNotEquals("model is unchanged?", collectionBasicModelOriginal, collectionBasicModelCopyEdited)
122→        assertEquals("model did not save?", testEditorModelEdited.toString().trim { it <= ' ' }, collectionBasicModelCopyEdited.toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
123→        assertTrue("model does not have our change?", collectionBasicModelCopyEdited.toString().contains(testModelQfmtEdit))
124→    }
125→
126→    @Test
127→    fun testDeleteTemplate() {
128→        val modelName = "Basic (and reversed card)"
129→
130→        // Start the CardTemplateEditor with a specific model, and make sure the model starts unchanged
131→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
132→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
133→        intent.putExtra("modelId", collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"))
134→        val templateEditorController = Robolectric.buildActivity(CardTemplateEditor::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
135→        saveControllerForCleanup(templateEditorController)
136→        val testEditor = templateEditorController.get()
137→        assertFalse("Model should not have changed yet", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
138→        assertEquals("Model should have 2 templates now", 2, testEditor.tempModel?.templateCount)
139→
140→        // Try to delete the template - click delete, click confirm for card delete, click confirm again for full sync
141→        val shadowTestEditor = shadowOf(testEditor)
142→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_delete))

213→    /**
214→     * In a model with two card templates using different fields, some notes may only use card 1,
215→     * and some may only use card 2. If you delete the 2nd template,
216→     * it will cause the notes that only use card 2 to disappear.
217→     *
218→     * So the unit test would then be to make a model like the "basic (optional reverse card)"
219→     * with two fields Enable1 and Enable2, and two templates "card 1" and "card 2".
220→     * Both cards use selective generation, so they're empty unless the corresponding field is set.
221→     *
222→     * So then in the unit test you make the model, add the two templates, then you add two notes,
223→     * with Enable1 and Enable2 respectively set to "y".
224→     * Then you try to delete one of the templates and it should fail
225→     *
226→     * (question: but I thought deleting one should work - still one card left to maintain the note,
227→     * and second template delete should fail since we finally get to a place where no cards are left?
228→     * I am having trouble creating selectively generated cards though - I can do one optional field but not 2 ugh)
229→     *
230→     */
231→    @Test
232→    fun testDeleteTemplateWithSelectivelyGeneratedCards() {
233→        val modelName = "Basic (optional reversed card)"
234→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
235→
236→        // Start the CardTemplateEditor with a specific model, and make sure the model starts unchanged
237→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
238→        intent.putExtra("modelId", collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"))
239→        val templateEditorController = Robolectric.buildActivity(CardTemplateEditor::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
240→        saveControllerForCleanup(templateEditorController)
241→        val testEditor = templateEditorController.get()
242→        assertFalse("Model should not have changed yet", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
243→        assertEquals("Model should have 2 templates now", 2, testEditor.tempModel?.templateCount)
244→        assertFalse("Ordinal pending add?", CardTemplateNotetype.isOrdinalPendingAdd(testEditor.tempModel!!, 0))
245→        assertFalse("Ordinal pending add?", CardTemplateNotetype.isOrdinalPendingAdd(testEditor.tempModel!!, 1))
246→
247→        // Try to delete Card 1 template - click delete, check confirm for card delete popup indicating it was possible, then dismiss it
248→        val shadowTestEditor = shadowOf(testEditor)
249→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_delete))
250→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
251→        assertEquals("Wrong dialog shown?", "Delete the “Card 1” card type, and its 0 cards?", getAlertDialogText(true))
252→        clickAlertDialogButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_NEGATIVE, true)
253→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
254→        assertFalse("Model should not have changed", testEditor.modelHasChanged())
255→
256→        // Create note with forward and back info, Add Reverse is empty, so should only be one card
257→        val selectiveGeneratedNote = col.newNote(collectionBasicModelOriginal)
258→        selectiveGeneratedNote.setField(0, "TestFront")
259→        selectiveGeneratedNote.setField(1, "TestBack")
260→        val fields = selectiveGeneratedNote.fields
261→        for (field in fields) {
262→            Timber.d("Got a field: %s", field)
263→        }
264→        col.addNote(selectiveGeneratedNote)
265→        assertEquals("selective generation should result in one card", 1, getModelCardCount(collectionBasicModelOriginal))
266→
267→        // Try to delete the template again, but there's selective generation means it would orphan the note
268→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_delete))
269→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
270→        assertEquals(
271→            "Did not show dialog about deleting only card?",
272→            getResourceString(R.string.card_template_editor_would_delete_note),
273→            getAlertDialogText(true)
274→        )
275→        clickAlertDialogButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE, true)
276→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
277→        assertNull("Can delete used template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(0)))
278→        assertEquals("Change already in database?", collectionBasicModelOriginal.toString().trim { it <= ' ' }, getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName).toString().trim { it <= ' ' })
279→        assertFalse("Ordinal pending add?", CardTemplateNotetype.isOrdinalPendingAdd(testEditor.tempModel!!, 0))
280→        assertEquals("Change incorrectly added to list?", 0, testEditor.tempModel?.templateChanges?.size)
281→
282→        // Assert can delete 'Card 2'
283→        assertNotNull("Cannot delete unused template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(1)))
284→
285→        // Edit note to have Add Reverse set to 'y' so we get a second card
286→        selectiveGeneratedNote.setField(2, "y")
287→        selectiveGeneratedNote.flush()
288→
289→        // - assert two cards
290→        assertEquals("should be two cards now", 2, getModelCardCount(collectionBasicModelOriginal))
291→
292→        // - assert can delete either Card template but not both
293→        assertNotNull("Cannot delete template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(0)))
294→        assertNotNull("Cannot delete template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(1)))
295→        assertNull("Can delete both templates?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(0, 1)))
296→
297→        // A couple more notes to make sure things are okay
298→        val secondNote = col.newNote(collectionBasicModelOriginal)
299→        secondNote.setField(0, "TestFront2")
300→        secondNote.setField(1, "TestBack2")
301→        secondNote.setField(2, "y")
302→        col.addNote(secondNote)
303→
304→        // - assert can delete either Card template but not both
305→        assertNotNull("Cannot delete template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(0)))
306→        assertNotNull("Cannot delete template?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(1)))
307→        assertNull("Can delete both templates?", col.notetypes.getCardIdsForModel(collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"), intArrayOf(0, 1)))
308→    }

533→        val modelName = "Basic"
534→
535→        // Start the CardTemplateEditor with a specific model, and make sure the model starts unchanged
536→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
537→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
538→        intent.putExtra("modelId", collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"))
539→        val templateEditorController = Robolectric.buildActivity(CardTemplateEditor::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
540→        saveControllerForCleanup(templateEditorController)
541→        val testEditor = templateEditorController.get()
542→
543→        // Change the model and make sure it registers as changed, but the database is unchanged
544→        val templateEditText = testEditor.findViewById<EditText>(R.id.editor_editText)
545→        val testModelQfmtEdit = "!@#$%^&*TEST*&^%$#@!"
546→        val updatedFrontContent = templateEditText.text.append(testModelQfmtEdit).toString()
547→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
548→        val cardTemplateFragment = testEditor.currentFragment
549→        val tempModel = testEditor.tempModel
550→        // set Bottom Navigation View to Style
551→        cardTemplateFragment!!.setCurrentEditorView(R.id.styling_edit, tempModel!!.css, R.string.card_template_editor_styling)
552→
553→        // set Bottom Navigation View to Front
554→        cardTemplateFragment.setCurrentEditorView(R.id.front_edit, tempModel.getTemplate(0).getString("qfmt"), R.string.card_template_editor_front)
555→
556→        // check if current content is updated or not
557→        assumeThat(templateEditText.text.toString(), Matchers.equalTo(updatedFrontContent))
558→    }
559→
560→    @Test
561→    fun testBottomNavigationViewLayoutTransition() {
562→        val modelName = "Basic"
563→
564→        // Start the CardTemplateEditor with a specific model, and make sure the model starts unchanged
565→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
566→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
567→        intent.putExtra("modelId", collectionBasicModelOriginal.getLong("id"))
568→        val templateEditorController = Robolectric.buildActivity(CardTemplateEditor::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
569→        saveControllerForCleanup(templateEditorController)
570→        val testEditor = templateEditorController.get()
571→
572→        // Change the model and make sure it registers as changed, but the database is unchanged
573→        val templateEditText = testEditor.findViewById<EditText>(R.id.editor_editText)
574→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
575→        val cardTemplateFragment = testEditor.currentFragment
576→        val tempModel = testEditor.tempModel
577→
578→        // check if current view is front(default) view
579→        assumeThat(templateEditText.text.toString(), Matchers.equalTo(tempModel!!.getTemplate(0).getString("qfmt")))
580→        assumeThat(cardTemplateFragment!!.currentEditorViewId, Matchers.equalTo(R.id.front_edit))
581→
582→        // set Bottom Navigation View to Style
583→        cardTemplateFragment.setCurrentEditorView(R.id.styling_edit, tempModel.css, R.string.card_template_editor_styling)
584→
585→        // check if current view is changed or not
586→        assumeThat(templateEditText.text.toString(), Matchers.equalTo(tempModel.css))
587→        assumeThat(cardTemplateFragment.currentEditorViewId, Matchers.equalTo(R.id.styling_edit))
588→    }
589→
590→    private fun addCardType(testEditor: CardTemplateEditor, shadowTestEditor: ShadowActivity) {
591→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_add))
592→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
593→        val ordinal = testEditor.viewPager.currentItem
594→        val numAffectedCards = if (!CardTemplateNotetype.isOrdinalPendingAdd(testEditor.tempModel!!, ordinal)) {
595→            col.notetypes.tmplUseCount(testEditor.tempModel!!.notetype, ordinal)
596→        } else {
597→            0
598→        }
599→        assertEquals(
600→            "Did not show dialog about adding template and it's card?",
601→            getQuantityString(R.plurals.card_template_editor_confirm_add, numAffectedCards, numAffectedCards),
602→            getAlertDialogText(true)
603→        )
604→        clickAlertDialogButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE, true)
605→    }
606→
607→    private fun getModelCardCount(notetype: NotetypeJson): Int {
608→        var cardCount = 0
609→        for (noteId in col.notetypes.nids(notetype)) {
610→            cardCount += col.getNote(noteId).numberOfCards()
611→        }
612→        return cardCount
613→    }
614→}

 17→@file:Suppress("unused")
 18→
 19→package com.ichi2.utils
 20→
 21→import android.content.DialogInterface
 22→import android.content.DialogInterface.OnClickListener
 23→import android.text.InputFilter
 24→import android.view.LayoutInflater
 25→import android.view.View
 26→import android.widget.Button
 27→import android.widget.CheckBox
 28→import android.widget.EditText
 29→import android.widget.FrameLayout
 30→import androidx.annotation.DrawableRes
 31→import androidx.annotation.StringRes
 32→import androidx.appcompat.app.AlertDialog
 33→import androidx.core.widget.doOnTextChanged
 34→import androidx.recyclerview.widget.LinearLayoutManager
 35→import androidx.recyclerview.widget.RecyclerView
 36→import com.google.android.material.textfield.TextInputLayout
 37→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 38→import com.ichi2.themes.Themes
 39→
 40→/** Wraps [DialogInterface.OnClickListener] as we don't need the `which` parameter */
 41→typealias DialogInterfaceListener = (DialogInterface) -> Unit
 42→
 43→fun DialogInterfaceListener.toClickListener(): OnClickListener {
 44→    return OnClickListener { dialog: DialogInterface, _ -> this(dialog) }
 45→}
 46→
 47→/*
 48→ * Allows easier transformations from [MaterialDialog] to [AlertDialog].
 49→ * Inline this file when material dialog is removed
 50→ */
 51→fun AlertDialog.Builder.title(@StringRes stringRes: Int? = null, text: String? = null): AlertDialog.Builder {
 52→    if (stringRes == null && text == null) {
 53→        throw IllegalArgumentException("either `stringRes` or `text` must be set")

156→ * Adds a checkbox to the dialog, whilst continuing to display the value of [message]
157→ * @param stringRes The string resource to display for the checkbox label.

159→ * @param isCheckedDefault Whether or not the checkbox is initially checked.
160→ * @param onToggle A listener invoked when the checkbox is checked or unchecked.
161→ */
162→fun AlertDialog.Builder.checkBoxPrompt(
163→    @StringRes stringRes: Int? = null,
164→    text: CharSequence? = null,
165→    isCheckedDefault: Boolean = false,
166→    onToggle: (checked: Boolean) -> Unit
167→): AlertDialog.Builder {

169→        throw IllegalArgumentException("either `stringRes` or `text` must be set")
170→    }

172→    val checkBox = checkBoxView.findViewById<CheckBox>(R.id.checkbox)
173→

175→    checkBox.text = checkBoxLabel
176→    checkBox.isChecked = isCheckedDefault
177→

212→    val recyclerView = LayoutInflater.from(context).inflate(R.layout.dialog_generic_recycler_view, null, false) as RecyclerView
213→    recyclerView.adapter = adapter
214→    recyclerView.layoutManager = LinearLayoutManager(context)
215→    this.setView(recyclerView)
216→}
217→
218→/**
219→ * @param hint The hint text to be displayed to the user
220→ * @param prefill The text to initially appear in the [EditText]
221→ * @param allowEmpty If true, [DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE] is disabled if the [EditText] is empty
222→ * @param displayKeyboard Whether to open the keyboard when the dialog appears
223→ * @param callback if [waitForPositiveButton], called when [positiveButton] is pressed, otherwise
224→ *  called whenever the text is changed
225→ * @param maxLength if set, the user may not enter more than the supplied number of digits
226→ * @param inputType see [EditText.setInputType]
227→ * @param waitForPositiveButton MaterialDialog compat: if `false` [callback] is called on input
228→ * if `true` [callback] is called when [positiveButton] is pressed
229→ */
230→fun AlertDialog.input(
231→    hint: String? = null,
232→    inputType: Int? = null,
233→    prefill: CharSequence? = null,
234→    allowEmpty: Boolean = false,
235→    maxLength: Int? = null,
236→    displayKeyboard: Boolean = false,
237→    waitForPositiveButton: Boolean = true,
238→    callback: (AlertDialog, CharSequence) -> Unit
239→): AlertDialog {
240→    // Builder.setView() may not be called before show()
241→    if (!this.isShowing) throw IllegalStateException("input() requires .show()")
242→
243→    getInputTextLayout().hint = hint
244→
245→    getInputField().apply {
246→        if (displayKeyboard) {
247→            AndroidUiUtils.setFocusAndOpenKeyboard(this, window!!)
248→        }
249→
250→        inputType?.let { this.inputType = it }
251→
252→        if (!waitForPositiveButton) {
253→            doOnTextChanged { text, _, _, _ ->
254→                callback(this@input, text ?: "")
255→            }
256→        } else {
257→            positiveButton.setOnClickListener { callback(this@input, this.text.toString()) }
258→        }
259→
260→        if (!allowEmpty) {
261→            // this is called after callback() so allowEmpty takes priority
262→            doOnTextChanged { text, _, _, _ ->
263→                if (waitForPositiveButton) {
264→                    // this is the only validation filter we apply - toggle on or off
265→                    this@input.positiveButton.isEnabled = !text.isNullOrEmpty()
266→                } else if (text.isNullOrEmpty()) {
267→                    // potentially other filters in `waitForPositiveButton`.
268→                    // WARN: this could be buggy as it does not toggle the button back on
269→                    this@input.positiveButton.isEnabled = false
270→                }
271→            }
272→        }
273→
274→        maxLength?.let { filters += InputFilter.LengthFilter(it) }
275→
276→        requestFocus()
277→        // this calls callback(this, prefill). positiveButton may be disabled if there's no prefill
278→        setText(prefill)
279→        moveCursorToEnd()
280→    }
281→    return this
282→}
283→
284→/**
285→ * @return the layout for the input text of the dialog
286→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the dialog does not contain [R.id.dialog_text_input_layout]]
287→ */
288→fun AlertDialog.getInputTextLayout() =
289→    requireNotNull(findViewById<TextInputLayout>(R.id.dialog_text_input_layout)) {
290→        "view must be dialog_generic_text_input"
291→    }
292→
293→/**
294→ * @return the [EditText] of the dialog
295→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the dialog does not contain [R.id.dialog_text_input_layout]]
296→ */
297→fun AlertDialog.getInputField() = getInputTextLayout().editText!!
298→
299→/** @see AlertDialog.getButton */
300→val AlertDialog.positiveButton: Button
301→    get() = getButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE)
302→

 39→object CollectionManager {
 40→    /**
 41→     * The currently active backend, which is created on demand via [ensureBackend], and
 42→     * implicitly via [ensureOpen] and routines like [withCol].
 43→     * The backend is long-lived, and will generally only be closed when switching interface
 44→     * languages or changing schema versions. A closed backend cannot be reused, and a new one
 45→     * must be created.
 46→     */
 47→    private var backend: Backend? = null
 48→
 49→    /**
 50→     * The current collection, which is opened on demand via [withCol]. If you need to
 51→     * close and reopen the collection in an atomic operation, add a new method that
 52→     * calls [withQueue], and then executes [ensureClosedInner] and [ensureOpenInner] inside it.
 53→     * A closed collection can be detected via [withOpenColOrNull] or by checking [Collection.dbClosed].
 54→     */
 55→    private var collection: Collection? = null
 56→
 57→    private var queue: CoroutineDispatcher = Dispatchers.IO.limitedParallelism(1)
 58→
 59→    @VisibleForTesting
 60→    var emulateOpenFailure = false
 61→
 62→    /** A speed optimisation to remove the logging function of the collection */
 63→    @VisibleForTesting
 64→    var disableLogFile: Boolean = false
 65→
 66→    /**
 67→     * Execute the provided block on a serial background queue, to ensure
 68→     * concurrent access does not happen.
 69→     *
 70→     * The background queue is run in a [Dispatchers.IO] context.
 71→     * It's important that the block is not suspendable - if it were, it would allow
 72→     * multiple requests to be interleaved when a suspend point was hit.
 73→     *
 74→     * TODO Disallow running functions that are supposed to be run inside the queue outside of it.
 75→     *   For instance, this can be done by marking a [block] with a context
 76→     *   that cannot be instantiated outside of this class:
 77→     *
 78→     *       suspend fun<T> withQueue(block: context(Queue) () -> T): T {
 79→     *          return withContext(collectionOperationsDispatcher) {
 80→     *              block(queue)
 81→     *          }
 82→     *      }
 83→     *
 84→     *   Then, only functions that are also marked can be run inside the block:
 85→     *
 86→     *       context(Queue) suspend fun canOnlyBeRunInWithQueue()
 87→     */
 88→    private suspend fun<T> withQueue(@WorkerThread block: CollectionManager.() -> T): T {
 89→        return withContext(queue) {
 90→            this@CollectionManager.block()
 91→        }
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    /**
 95→     * Execute the provided block with the collection, opening if necessary.
 96→     *
 97→     * Calls are serialized, and run in background [Dispatchers.IO] thread.
 98→     *
 99→     * Parallel calls to this function are guaranteed to be serialized, so you can be
100→     * sure the collection won't be closed or modified by another thread. This guarantee

content.text:
The "discard changes" dialog in Anki-Android was improved by updating its options to be more intuitive, matching the style used in Anki Desktop and Google Photos. Instead of generic "OK" and "Cancel" buttons, the dialog now uses "Discard" and "Keep editing", with a more descriptive message.

### Changes implemented:

1.  **`DiscardChangesDialog.kt`**:
    *   Converted the class to a Kotlin `object` for simpler access.
    *   Updated the `showDialog` method to accept custom strings for the positive button, negative button, and the dialog message.
    *   Switched to using the `message`, `positiveButton`, and `negativeButton` extension functions that accept raw strings.

2.  **`02-strings.xml`**:
    *   Ensured the `<string name="discard">Discard</string>` string is available.
    *   Removed the outdated `discard_unsaved_changes` string.

3.  **Call sites updated**:
    Updated the following files to use the new `showDialog` signature, providing localized strings from the backend (`CollectionManager.TR`) and the new `discard` resource:
    *   `NoteEditor.kt`
    *   `CardTemplateEditor.kt`
    *   `CardTemplateBrowserAppearanceEditor.kt`
    *   `EditDeckDescriptionDialog.kt`
    *   `DeckOptions.kt`

The new dialog now consistently uses:
- **Message**: "Discard current input?" (via `TR.addingDiscardCurrentInput()`)
- **Positive Button**: "Discard" (via `R.string.discard`)
- **Negative Button**: "Keep editing" (via `TR.addingKeepEditing()`)

Existing tests were not modified to adhere to project restrictions, although it's noted that some tests might need updates to match the new localized strings in a real-world development scenario.

Summary of production changes:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/DiscardChangesDialog.kt` updated.
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/NoteEditor.kt` updated.
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateEditor.kt` updated.
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateBrowserAppearanceEditor.kt` updated.
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/EditDeckDescriptionDialog.kt` updated.
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/DeckOptions.kt` updated.
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/02-strings.xml` updated.

218→     * So the unit test would then be to make a model like the "basic (optional reverse card)"
219→     * with two fields Enable1 and Enable2, and two templates "card 1" and "card 2".
220→     * Both cards use selective generation, so they're empty unless the corresponding field is set.
221→     *
222→     * So then in the unit test you make the model, add the two templates, then you add two notes,

224→     * Then you try to delete one of the templates and it should fail

590→    private fun addCardType(testEditor: CardTemplateEditor, shadowTestEditor: ShadowActivity) {
591→        assertTrue("Unable to click?", shadowTestEditor.clickMenuItem(R.id.action_add))
592→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
593→        val ordinal = testEditor.viewPager.currentItem
594→        val numAffectedCards = if (!CardTemplateNotetype.isOrdinalPendingAdd(testEditor.tempModel!!, ordinal)) {
595→            col.notetypes.tmplUseCount(testEditor.tempModel!!.notetype, ordinal)
596→        } else {
597→            0
598→        }
599→        assertEquals(
600→            "Did not show dialog about adding template and it's card?",
601→            getQuantityString(R.plurals.card_template_editor_confirm_add, numAffectedCards, numAffectedCards),
602→            getAlertDialogText(true)
603→        )
604→        clickAlertDialogButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE, true)
605→    }
606→
607→    private fun getModelCardCount(notetype: NotetypeJson): Int {
608→        var cardCount = 0
609→        for (noteId in col.notetypes.nids(notetype)) {
610→            cardCount += col.getNote(noteId).numberOfCards()
611→        }
612→        return cardCount
613→    }

 40→/** Wraps [DialogInterface.OnClickListener] as we don't need the `which` parameter */
 41→typealias DialogInterfaceListener = (DialogInterface) -> Unit
 42→
 43→fun DialogInterfaceListener.toClickListener(): OnClickListener {
 44→    return OnClickListener { dialog: DialogInterface, _ -> this(dialog) }
 45→}
 46→
 47→/*
 48→ * Allows easier transformations from [MaterialDialog] to [AlertDialog].
 49→ * Inline this file when material dialog is removed
 50→ */
 51→fun AlertDialog.Builder.title(@StringRes stringRes: Int? = null, text: String? = null): AlertDialog.Builder {
 52→    if (stringRes == null && text == null) {
 53→        throw IllegalArgumentException("either `stringRes` or `text` must be set")
 54→    }
 55→    return if (stringRes != null) {
 56→        setTitle(stringRes)
 57→    } else {
 58→        setTitle(text)
 59→    }
 60→}
 61→
 62→fun AlertDialog.Builder.message(@StringRes stringRes: Int? = null, text: CharSequence? = null): AlertDialog.Builder {
 63→    if (stringRes == null && text == null) {
 64→        throw IllegalArgumentException("either `stringRes` or `text` must be set")
 65→    }
 66→    return if (stringRes != null) {
 67→        setMessage(stringRes)
 68→    } else {
 69→        setMessage(text)
 70→    }
 71→}
 72→
 73→/**
 74→ * Shows an icon to the left of the dialog title.
 75→ */
 76→fun AlertDialog.Builder.iconAttr(
 77→    @DrawableRes res: Int
 78→) = apply {
 79→    return this.setIcon(Themes.getResFromAttr(this.context, res))
 80→}
 81→
 82→fun AlertDialog.Builder.positiveButton(
 83→    @StringRes stringRes: Int? = null,
 84→    text: CharSequence? = null,
 85→    click: DialogInterfaceListener? = null
 86→): AlertDialog.Builder {
 87→    if (stringRes == null && text == null) {
 88→        throw IllegalArgumentException("either `stringRes` or `text` must be set")
 89→    }
 90→    return if (stringRes != null) {
 91→        this.setPositiveButton(stringRes, click?.toClickListener())
 92→    } else {
 93→        this.setPositiveButton(text, click?.toClickListener())
 94→    }
 95→}
 96→
 97→fun AlertDialog.Builder.neutralButton(
 98→    @StringRes stringRes: Int? = null,
 99→    text: CharSequence? = null,
100→    click: DialogInterfaceListener? = null
101→): AlertDialog.Builder {
102→    if (stringRes == null && text == null) {
103→        throw IllegalArgumentException("either `stringRes` or `text` must be set")
104→    }
105→    return if (stringRes != null) {
106→        this.setNeutralButton(stringRes, click?.toClickListener())
107→    } else {
108→        this.setNeutralButton(text, click?.toClickListener())
109→    }
110→}

127→fun AlertDialog.Builder.cancelable(cancelable: Boolean): AlertDialog.Builder {
128→    return this.setCancelable(cancelable)
129→}

131→/**
132→ * Executes the provided block, then creates an [AlertDialog] with the arguments supplied
133→ * and immediately displays the dialog
134→ */
135→inline fun AlertDialog.Builder.show(block: AlertDialog.Builder.() -> Unit): AlertDialog {
136→    this.apply { block() }
137→    return this.show()
138→}
139→
140→/**
141→ * Creates an [AlertDialog] from the [AlertDialog.Builder] instance, then executes [block] with it.
142→ */
143→fun AlertDialog.Builder.createAndApply(block: AlertDialog.() -> Unit): AlertDialog = create().apply {
144→    block()
145→}
146→
147→/**
148→ * Executes [block] on the [AlertDialog.Builder] instance and returns the initialized [AlertDialog].
149→ */
150→fun AlertDialog.Builder.create(block: AlertDialog.Builder.() -> Unit): AlertDialog {
151→    block()
152→    return create()
153→}

155→/**
156→ * Adds a checkbox to the dialog, whilst continuing to display the value of [message]
157→ * @param stringRes The string resource to display for the checkbox label.
158→ * @param text The literal string to display for the checkbox label.
159→ * @param isCheckedDefault Whether or not the checkbox is initially checked.
160→ * @param onToggle A listener invoked when the checkbox is checked or unchecked.
161→ */
162→fun AlertDialog.Builder.checkBoxPrompt(
163→    @StringRes stringRes: Int? = null,
164→    text: CharSequence? = null,
165→    isCheckedDefault: Boolean = false,
166→    onToggle: (checked: Boolean) -> Unit
167→): AlertDialog.Builder {
168→    if (stringRes == null && text == null) {
169→        throw IllegalArgumentException("either `stringRes` or `text` must be set")
170→    }
171→    val checkBoxView = View.inflate(this.context, R.layout.alert_dialog_checkbox, null)
172→    val checkBox = checkBoxView.findViewById<CheckBox>(R.id.checkbox)
173→
174→    val checkBoxLabel = if (stringRes != null) context.getString(stringRes) else text
175→    checkBox.text = checkBoxLabel
176→    checkBox.isChecked = isCheckedDefault
177→
178→    checkBox.setOnCheckedChangeListener { _, isChecked ->
179→        onToggle(isChecked)
180→    }
181→
182→    return this.setView(checkBoxView)
183→}
184→
185→fun AlertDialog.getCheckBoxPrompt(): CheckBox =
186→    requireNotNull(findViewById(R.id.checkbox)) {
187→        "CheckBox prompt is not available. Forgot to call AlertDialog.Builder.checkBoxPrompt()?"
188→    }
189→
190→fun AlertDialog.Builder.customView(
191→    view: View,
192→    paddingTop: Int = 0,
193→    paddingBottom: Int = 0,
194→    paddingLeft: Int = 0,
195→    paddingRight: Int = 0
196→): AlertDialog.Builder {
197→    val container = FrameLayout(context)
198→
199→    val containerParams = FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
200→        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
201→        FrameLayout.LayoutParams.WRAP_CONTENT
202→    )
203→
204→    container.setPadding(paddingLeft, paddingTop, paddingRight, paddingBottom)
205→    container.addView(view, containerParams)
206→    setView(container)
207→
208→    return this
209→}
210→
211→fun AlertDialog.Builder.customListAdapter(adapter: RecyclerView.Adapter<*>) {
212→    val recyclerView = LayoutInflater.from(context).inflate(R.layout.dialog_generic_recycler_view, null, false) as RecyclerView
213→    recyclerView.adapter = adapter
214→    recyclerView.layoutManager = LinearLayoutManager(context)
215→    this.setView(recyclerView)
216→}

218→/**
219→ * @param hint The hint text to be displayed to the user
220→ * @param prefill The text to initially appear in the [EditText]
221→ * @param allowEmpty If true, [DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE] is disabled if the [EditText] is empty
222→ * @param displayKeyboard Whether to open the keyboard when the dialog appears
223→ * @param callback if [waitForPositiveButton], called when [positiveButton] is pressed, otherwise
224→ *  called whenever the text is changed
225→ * @param maxLength if set, the user may not enter more than the supplied number of digits
226→ * @param inputType see [EditText.setInputType]
227→ * @param waitForPositiveButton MaterialDialog compat: if `false` [callback] is called on input
228→ * if `true` [callback] is called when [positiveButton] is pressed
229→ */
230→fun AlertDialog.input(
231→    hint: String? = null,
232→    inputType: Int? = null,
233→    prefill: CharSequence? = null,
234→    allowEmpty: Boolean = false,
235→    maxLength: Int? = null,
236→    displayKeyboard: Boolean = false,
237→    waitForPositiveButton: Boolean = true,
238→    callback: (AlertDialog, CharSequence) -> Unit
239→): AlertDialog {
240→    // Builder.setView() may not be called before show()
241→    if (!this.isShowing) throw IllegalStateException("input() requires .show()")
242→
243→    getInputTextLayout().hint = hint
244→
245→    getInputField().apply {
246→        if (displayKeyboard) {
247→            AndroidUiUtils.setFocusAndOpenKeyboard(this, window!!)
248→        }
249→
250→        inputType?.let { this.inputType = it }
251→
252→        if (!waitForPositiveButton) {
253→            doOnTextChanged { text, _, _, _ ->
254→                callback(this@input, text ?: "")
255→            }
256→        } else {
257→            positiveButton.setOnClickListener { callback(this@input, this.text.toString()) }
258→        }
259→
260→        if (!allowEmpty) {
261→            // this is called after callback() so allowEmpty takes priority
262→            doOnTextChanged { text, _, _, _ ->
263→                if (waitForPositiveButton) {
264→                    // this is the only validation filter we apply - toggle on or off
265→                    this@input.positiveButton.isEnabled = !text.isNullOrEmpty()
266→                } else if (text.isNullOrEmpty()) {
267→                    // potentially other filters in `waitForPositiveButton`.
268→                    // WARN: this could be buggy as it does not toggle the button back on
269→                    this@input.positiveButton.isEnabled = false
270→                }
271→            }
272→        }
273→
274→        maxLength?.let { filters += InputFilter.LengthFilter(it) }
275→
276→        requestFocus()
277→        // this calls callback(this, prefill). positiveButton may be disabled if there's no prefill
278→        setText(prefill)
279→        moveCursorToEnd()
280→    }
281→    return this
282→}

284→/**
285→ * @return the layout for the input text of the dialog
286→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the dialog does not contain [R.id.dialog_text_input_layout]]
287→ */
288→fun AlertDialog.getInputTextLayout() =
289→    requireNotNull(findViewById<TextInputLayout>(R.id.dialog_text_input_layout)) {
290→        "view must be dialog_generic_text_input"
291→    }

293→/**
294→ * @return the [EditText] of the dialog
295→ * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the dialog does not contain [R.id.dialog_text_input_layout]]
296→ */
297→fun AlertDialog.getInputField() = getInputTextLayout().editText!!

300→val AlertDialog.positiveButton: Button
301→    get() = getButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE)

 39→object CollectionManager {
 40→    /**
 41→     * The currently active backend, which is created on demand via [ensureBackend], and
 42→     * implicitly via [ensureOpen] and routines like [withCol].
 43→     * The backend is long-lived, and will generally only be closed when switching interface
 44→     * languages or changing schema versions. A closed backend cannot be reused, and a new one
 45→     * must be created.
 46→     */
 47→    private var backend: Backend? = null
 48→
 49→    /**
 50→     * The current collection, which is opened on demand via [withCol]. If you need to
 51→     * close and reopen the collection in an atomic operation, add a new method that
 52→     * calls [withQueue], and then executes [ensureClosedInner] and [ensureOpenInner] inside it.
 53→     * A closed collection can be detected via [withOpenColOrNull] or by checking [Collection.dbClosed].
 54→     */
 55→    private var collection: Collection? = null
 56→
 57→    private var queue: CoroutineDispatcher = Dispatchers.IO.limitedParallelism(1)
 58→
 59→    @VisibleForTesting
 60→    var emulateOpenFailure = false
 61→
 62→    /** A speed optimisation to remove the logging function of the collection */
 63→    @VisibleForTesting
 64→    var disableLogFile: Boolean = false
 65→
 66→    /**
 67→     * Execute the provided block on a serial background queue, to ensure
 68→     * concurrent access does not happen.
 69→     *
 70→     * The background queue is run in a [Dispatchers.IO] context.
 71→     * It's important that the block is not suspendable - if it were, it would allow
 72→     * multiple requests to be interleaved when a suspend point was hit.
 73→     *
 74→     * TODO Disallow running functions that are supposed to be run inside the queue outside of it.
 75→     *   For instance, this can be done by marking a [block] with a context
 76→     *   that cannot be instantiated outside of this class:
 77→     *
 78→     *       suspend fun<T> withQueue(block: context(Queue) () -> T): T {
 79→     *          return withContext(collectionOperationsDispatcher) {
 80→     *              block(queue)
 81→     *          }
 82→     *      }
 83→     *
 84→     *   Then, only functions that are also marked can be run inside the block:
 85→     *
 86→     *       context(Queue) suspend fun canOnlyBeRunInWithQueue()
 87→     */
 88→    private suspend fun<T> withQueue(@WorkerThread block: CollectionManager.() -> T): T {
 89→        return withContext(queue) {
 90→            this@CollectionManager.block()
 91→        }
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    /**
 95→     * Execute the provided block with the collection, opening if necessary.
 96→     *
 97→     * Calls are serialized, and run in background [Dispatchers.IO] thread.
 98→     *
 99→     * Parallel calls to this function are guaranteed to be serialized, so you can be
100→     * sure the collection won't be closed or modified by another thread. This guarantee
